very_formal.use_when: Speaking to C-suite, legal matters, first contact with authority figure

professional.use_when: Most business communication, upward to manager, first-time contacts

warm_professional.use_when: Colleagues you know well, established relationships, networking

casual.use_when: Close peers, friends in professional setting, internal team

very_casual.use_when: Very close collaborators, friend-like relationships, internal culture allows it

urgent.use_when: Time-sensitive matter, deadline approaching, immediate action needed

grateful.use_when: Thank yous, recognitions, appreciating someone's help
grateful.opening: Name the specific thing you're thankful for

earnest.use_when: Genuine conversations, sincere requests, real interest
earnest.opening: Be direct about why you're reaching out

enthusiastic.use_when: Announcements, exciting news, course launches, celebrations
enthusiastic.opening: Lead with the benefit or exciting news

neutral.use_when: Informational emails, status updates, routine communication

empathetic.use_when: Support emails, apologies, difficult situations, customer issues

firm.use_when: Setting boundaries, saying no, redirecting, enforcing policy

collaborative.use_when: Team communication, partnership proposals, collaborative asks

unclear_cta.mistake: Reader doesn't know what you want them to do
unclear_cta.example: Would love to catch up sometime!
unclear_cta.fix: Would you have 30 minutes for coffee next week? I'm free Tuesday-Thursday afternoons.

generic_opening.fix: Your talk on AI agents in customer service changed how I'm thinking about this problem

burying_the_lede.example: [Long story], oh and by the way, we need approval by Friday
burying_the_lede.fix: We need board approval by Friday. Here's the situation...

too_many_asks.mistake: Multiple asks in one email = lower response rate
too_many_asks.example: Can you also send me that report? And introduce me to [Person]? And review this draft?
too_many_asks.fix: One email per ask. Prioritize what's most important.

unclear_deadline.mistake: Reader doesn't know when you need a response
unclear_deadline.example: Let me know what you think
unclear_deadline.fix: Can you get back to me by EOD Thursday?

defensive_tone_in_apology.example: I apologize but I was really busy that day
defensive_tone_in_apology.fix: I apologize for missing that deadline. I should have communicated sooner.

vague_in_proposal.mistake: Reader doesn't understand what you're proposing
vague_in_proposal.example: Would love to explore a partnership
vague_in_proposal.fix: I think we could create value by [specific idea]. Specifically, we'd [concrete outcome].

wrong_formality_level.mistake: Too formal with close colleague or too casual with executive
wrong_formality_level.example: Submitting formal memo to close teammate

no_acknowledgment_of_recipient.mistake: Email is all about you, nothing about them
no_acknowledgment_of_recipient.example: Here's what I want to talk about...
no_acknowledgment_of_recipient.fix: I know you're working on X. I think we could solve Y together.
